items.headline: The 'Dirty Dozen' is the new 'Mag 7': Will OpenAI or Anthropic take the last spot?
items.summary: The "Dirty Dozen" refers to all the stocks in the "Magnificent Seven," as well as SpaceX (SPCX), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), Broadcom (AVGO), and Micron (MU). The final spot will go to either OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) or Anthropic (ANTH.PVT). Barron's associate editor Al Root, Yahoo Finance Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley, and Morning Brief host Julie Hyman discuss more in the video above.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: The Dow Is Above 52,000. It's Only Been 12 Days Since It Hit 51,000.
items.summary: The Dow crossed the 52,000 threshold on a day when tech was holding back the S&P 500 and Nasdaq. The blue-chip index was up 440 points to about 52,115. Any close higher would be a record close after yesterday's milestone, but a close above 52,000 would mark just 12 days since it hit 51,000.
items.why_it_matters: News appears more supportive of sentiment or growth expectations.

items.headline: Caterpillar vs. Komatsu: Which Heavy Equipment Stock is the Better Buy?

items.headline: How Does the Belvidere Buyout Enhance Generac's Generator Portfolio?
items.summary: GNRC expands C&I power capabilities with a new Illinois facility, boosting large-generator output and supporting rising demand across industries.

items.headline: 2 Reasons to Watch CAT and 1 to Stay Cautious
items.summary: Caterpillar has been on fire lately. In the past six months alone, the company’s stock price has rocketed 58.6%, reaching $933.99 per share. This was partly due to its solid quarterly results, and the performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.
